ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_performance" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record */,
  "messages_sent" INTEGER /* Total number of messages sent in the time period */,
  "latency_per_operation" REAL /* Average latency per operation (in seconds) */,
  "overhead_data_structure" INTEGER /* Size of overhead data structure (in bytes) */,
  "overhead_per_message" REAL /* Overhead incurred per message (in bytes) */,
  "date_collected" TEXT /* Date when the data was collected */,
  "time_collected" TEXT /* Time when the data was collected */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the network node */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the network node */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_reconfigurations" (
  "reconfig_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reconfiguration record */,
  "number_of_reconfigurations" INTEGER /* Total number of reconfigurations performed */,
  "total_reconfiguration_time" REAL /* Total time taken for reconfigurations (in seconds) */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the corresponding performance record */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the network node */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the network node */,
  "reconfig_details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the reconfiguration changes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reconfig_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_reconfigurations_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES network_performance ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., network engineer, analyst, admin) */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's role */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., latency, message loss) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Detailed message describing the alert */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the corresponding performance record */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the network node */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the network node */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the alert has been resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES network_performance ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical data record */,
  "messages_sent" INTEGER /* Total number of messages sent */,
  "latency_per_operation" REAL /* Average latency per operation */,
  "date_recorded" TEXT /* Date when the data was recorded */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the network node */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the network node */,
  "throughput" REAL /* Throughput of the network */,
  "overhead_data_structure" INTEGER /* Size of overhead data structure */,
  "overhead_per_message" REAL /* Overhead incurred per message */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., latency, throughput) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric and its significance */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_logs" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration change */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change made */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was applied */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User who made the change */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the network node */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the network node */,
  "change_time" TEXT /* Time when the change was applied */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., alert threshold) */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the setting value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role and its responsibilities */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User providing the feedback */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback */,
  "date_submitted" TEXT /* Date the feedback was submitted */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the network node */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the network node */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the feedback has been addressed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
